rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROSIA L. HART REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Both the trustee and her counsel must appear. Issues may include (but not limited to): whether this petition should be taken off calendar for lack of progress and whether the trustee should be suspended for continued failure to address the issues. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LAURENCE B. KNOWLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/21/2021. 1) The account ends 11/30/2020. Please file statements that verify the balance at the end of the account period. 2) Please explain the calculation of bond alleging anticipated income of $10,000.00 when this account shows income of $61,319.34. Please explain the legal basis for using anticipated income in bond calculations. 3) Please explain when the duplicate payment of fees was made to conservator and paid back to the estate. Please explain the legal basis for not paying interest on the amount. 4) Conservator timesheets must be amended to include the total fee charged for each entry. Time records must identify the billing party providing the service and include the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. [LRSF 14.53]. 5) On Schedule C Disbursements please explain the need for conservatee to have a cellphone/landline. See entries for AT&T/Cell phone and AT&T/Telephone. 6) On Schedule C Disbursements please provide receipts for the following entries: a. 12/31/2019 Home Instead Senior Care $19,196.00 12/31/2019 Home Instead Care for $18,614.00. b. 10/17/2019 Home Instead Senior Care for $1,112.63. c. 12/31/2019 Olive Garden/Dining: holiday event for $242.29. d. 12/2/2019 AC Heating and Cooling Service, Inc./Repairs for $7,849.00. 7) On Schedule C Disbursements please explain the legal basis for Debra J. Dolch being paid $23,725.00 when the 1/15/2020 order authorized $23,700.25. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/7/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD GORDON SELF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/28/21. (No earlier availability.) What happened to the truck? There were no sales. It appears that, if actual distributions received from the Recology ESOP were greater than the I&A value, the increase was due to receipts. If the statutory compensation is to be based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules must be filed. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] Furthermore, the explanation in paragraph 15 is impossible to follow. How did the administrator arrive at "two thirds of the payment" (what payment?) having a "net value of $241,525.19?" What is meant by the "gross value of the distribution to tax withholding was $309,647.67?" And how do those amounts fit with the purported "gain over the $223,436.56 appraised value of 3/5ths of the assets?" What do these fractions mean? There is no Exhibit G attached to the amended petition. The exhibit marked Exhibit C appears to be the proposed distribution. Paragraph 7 of the amended petition shows assets on hand to be $50,728.77, not $42,994.31. Paragraph 22 of the amended petition shows two different figures, above which the three heirs would receive equally. The administrator was appointed on 5/3/2017. The order was filed on 5/4/17. The order for preliminary distribution was dated 10/16/18. It was filed on 10/17/18. The creditors' claims period expired on 9/5/17, not 12/21/16. After these issues are addressed in a second amended petition, an appearance will likely be required. Courtesy copies must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/18/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LESLIE ALCALDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/15/21. No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A.] Original bank statements must be filed to verify the ending balance in an accounting. Whenever originals are E-filed, the attorney or the fiduciary who retains the original must file a declaration certifying that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under Local Rule 2.11 M.4., which provides that the original(s) must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." Courtesy copies and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/4/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EMMA EDWARDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed: 1) The executor hired his own sons to help clean out the decedent's residence at an hourly rate of $20, for a total of $822.36, without prior Court approval. As an online legal dictionary notes, "A fiduciary is held to a standard of conduct and trust above that of a stranger or of a casual business person. He must avoid "self-dealing" or "conflicts of interests" in which the potential benefit to the fiduciary is in conflict with what is best for the person who trusts him." 2) The proof of service filed on 4/28/21 is incomplete at item 4a (date of mailing). Does the Court wish to waive the defective incomplete proof of service? 3) The report at paragraph 11, page 3, says that the executor "liquidated the decedent[']s bank and investment assets" but only three bank accounts and the decedent's residence were inventoried. The Court might wish to confirm that "investment assets" refer only to the real property, not a brokerage account or other such investment. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: LAURENCE B. KNOWLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, First Accounting of Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/21/2021. 1) Please explain the calculation of bond alleging anticipated income of $46,000.00 when this account shows income of $52,984.53. Please explain the legal basis for using anticipated income in bond calculations. 2) On Schedule C Disbursements please provide receipts for the following entries: a. 10/9/20 HDIS/Personal care supplies (10/1/2020) for $266.45 b. 10/15/20 HDIS/Personal care supplies (Inv. #18388518; 10/2/2020) for $266.45. 3) On Schedule C Disbursements please explain the need for beneficiary to have a cellphone/landline. See entries for AT&T/Cell phone, AT&T/Telephone, AT&T Mobility/Telephone. 4) On Schedule C please explain entry 5/19/2020 Digital Life AT&T for security for $66.87. 5) On Schedule C Disbursements please explain the legal basis for the trust paying for entry 5/14/2020 Martin W. Harris account preparation (Supplemental Account in the Barton H. and Phyllis D. Knowles Trust. 6) Trustee timesheets must be amended to include the total fee charged for each entry. Time records must identify the billing party providing the service and include the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. [LRSF 14.53]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/7/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
